title: Pleuropogon californicus
taxonomy: Pleuropogon californicus is classified within the family Restionaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as restios. This family is part of the larger clade known as Poales, which includes several orders of flowering plants. The order Poales is a group of monocots, characterized by having a single cotyledon in their seeds. Pleuropogon californicus is a species of sedge, and as such, it is also classified within the larger clade known as Liliopsida, or monocotyledons. The classification of Pleuropogon californicus reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Poales clade.
uses: Pleuropogon californicus, also known as California fescue, is a native grass species to the Pacific coast of North America. While limited information is available on its medicinal uses, it has been traditionally used by indigenous communities for various purposes. benefits: Pleuropogon californicus, a species of grass, is traditionally used in folk medicine. properties: Pleuropogon californicus, also known as California spike-rush, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine.
